title: APIv2: containers/create does not call Init
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

creating a container via the API does create the container, but isn't created  in the OCI runtime
thus attaching to the container does not work.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. create a container via the api

2. try to attach to the created container

**Describe the results you received:**

attach works

**Describe the results you expected:**

`Request Failed(Internal Server Error): can only attach to created or running containers: container state improper `

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.8.1-dev
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.17
Git Commit:         1bed53b02c2f65ba26dc320a797c6fe473fce9a1-dirty
Built:              Wed Feb 19 11:14:11 2020
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: 1bed53b02c2f65ba26dc320a797c6fe473fce9a1-dirty
  go version: go1.12.17
  podman version: 1.8.1-dev
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.13.1
  CgroupVersion: v1
  Conmon:
    package: 'conmon: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon'
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.10, commit: unknown'
  Distribution:
    distribution: debian
    version: "10"
  MemFree: 735125504
  MemTotal: 4138446848
  OCIRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: 'runc: /usr/sbin/runc'
    path: /usr/sbin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0~rc6+dfsg1
      commit: 1.0.0~rc6+dfsg1-3
      spec: 1.0.1
  SwapFree: 2897207296
  SwapTotal: 4138442752
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 2
  eventlogger: journald
  hostname: hst-m04.dc.burg-1.de
  kernel: 4.19.0-6-amd64
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 1981h 3m 39.01s (Approximately 82.54 days)

  search:
  - docker.io
  - quay.io
store:
  ConfigFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 3
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions: {}
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 25
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  VolumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes
```

i have implemented ctr.Init before returning the container response in CreateContainer, that seems to do the trick. I am not sure tho if that's the desired way to do it.
If it is, please let me know. and I will open a PR.

comments.body: This looks like an typo in the error message `can only attach to created or running containers: container state improper`; we can only attach to running containers. @mheon WDYT?

the error is fine, attaching to created containers too.
I've fixed it with

```
if err := ctr.Init(r.Context()); err != nil {
		utils.Error(w, "Something went wrong.", http.StatusInternalServerError, errors.Wrap(err, "ContainerInit()"))
		return
	}
```

above https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/pkg/api/handlers/containers_create.go#L75

and attaching via the api, works like I expected it to.
i am however not sure if that's the intended design

to add: it feels like the container is not full created but instead only configured and there seems to be no other api method to finish creating it. things interacting with the API expect the container to be in the created state, instead of configured.

Init should happen automatically in start - see `prepareToStart()` (https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/libpod/container_internal.go#L767-L777). What we're probably seeing here is an incorrect state check somewhere in the handler code, which an Init is working around by changing the state of the container.

As I have understood It this is somewhat replicating the docker api and from what I gathered from things like gitlab-runner it expects the container to be fully created as it attachtes right after to inject some things and then starts the container with a custom command. 

With the lines above gitlab-runner does get pretty far now, not all the way there yet but it can create, pull, attach, kill and stop. 

Maybe @baude can give more insight.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@schubter @mheon Is this still an issue, or should it be closed?
yes, created containers via the api cannot be interacted with, ie attached to as they are not initialized in the runtime.

I am unable to get this behavior to work with Docker. Both Podman and Docker behave the same way (cannot attach to container when using the API).

I ran this command using Docker 1.13 and the container cannot be attached
```
curl --unix-socket /var/run/docker.sock -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"Image": "docker.io/library/busybox:latest"}' \
  -X POST http:/v1.24/containers/create?name=busybox

curl --unix-socket /var/run/docker.sock -X POST http:/v1.24/containers/busybox/start
```

I have different example where APIv2 behaves differently compared to docker / CLI

Docker
CLI
```
docker create --interactive --tty -p 61616:61616 --name activemq docker.io/rmohr/activemq:5.15.9-alpine
docker start activemq
sleep 10
docker logs activemq
docker inspect activemq > /tmp/test-activemq-docker.txt
docker stop activemq
docker rm activemq 
```

REST

```
curl --unix-socket /var/run/docker.sock -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" 'http://127.0.0.1:8880/containers/create?name=activemq' --data '{"name":"activemq", "Image":"docker.io/rmohr/activemq:5.15.9-alpine", "ExposedPorts": { "61616/tcp": {} }, "HostConfig": { "PortBindings": { "61616/tcp": [{ "HostPort": "" }] } } }'
curl --unix-socket /var/run/docker.sock -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" 'http://127.0.0.1:8880/containers/activemq/start'
sleep 10
curl --unix-socket /var/run/docker.sock --output - -H "Content-Type: application/json" "http://127.0.0.1:8880/containers/activemq/logs?stdout=true&stderr=true"
docker logs activemq
docker inspect activemq > /tmp/test-activemq-docker-api.txt
curl --unix-socket /var/run/docker.sock -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" 'http://127.0.0.1:8880/containers/activemq/stop'
curl --unix-socket /var/run/docker.sock -X DELETE -H "Content-Type: application/json" 'http://127.0.0.1:8880/containers/activemq'
```

Podman
CLI

```
podman create --interactive --tty -p 61616:61616 --name activemq docker.io/rmohr/activemq:5.15.9-alpine
podman start activemq
sleep 10
podman logs activemq
podman inspect activemq > /tmp/test-activemq-docker.txt
podman stop activemq
podman rm activemq 
```

REST

```
podman --log-level=debug system service -t 0 tcp:localhost:8880 2>&1>/tmp/podman-rest.log &

curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" 'http://127.0.0.1:8880/containers/create?name=activemq' --data '{"name":"activemq", "Image":"docker.io/rmohr/activemq:5.15.9-alpine", "ExposedPorts": { "61616/tcp": {} }, "HostConfig": { "PortBindings": { "61616/tcp": [{ "HostPort": "" }] } } }'
curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" 'http://127.0.0.1:8880/containers/activemq/start'
sleep 10
curl --output - -H "Content-Type: application/json" "http://127.0.0.1:8880/containers/activemq/logs?stdout=true&stderr=true"
podman logs activemq
podman inspect activemq > /tmp/test-activemq-docker-api.txt
curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" 'http://127.0.0.1:8880/containers/activemq/stop'
curl -X DELETE -H "Content-Type: application/json" 'http://127.0.0.1:8880/containers/activemq'
```


Please notice that REST log contains only "/bin/sh: bin/activemq: not found" instead of "INFO | ..." in other cases.
@mheon Please would you check the case in the above comment ? It blocks use of testcontainers with podman
How does it behave differently? Can you provide details?

It is described in the comment https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/5260#issuecomment-668127624 - it contains full reproducer.
Basically the container does not start properly because working directory is not set (and possibly other few things)
Actually you check just by running "Podman CLI" part and "Podman REST" part, docker is not needed.

I'm 99% sure that the original issue here (Init not being called) has been resolved. These sound like separate issues?

Can you file separate issues for each thing you're seeing?
Sure, I'll do, thanx for looking into it
I'm going to close this one - Init is definitely called as part of start now.
